Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to convert a raw Windows activity system log into a structured event log suitable for process mining This involves defining Case IDs Activity Names Timestamps and potentially other attributes while providing a rationale for the transformation logic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Format CSV like structure Content User interactions with various applications Word Chrome Acrobat Excel and specific windows documents emails Actions FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE Timeline A single morning session on 2024 12 11 08 59 to 09 08

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analysis of the Log The user starts with Quarterly Report docx but immediately switches to Document1 docx They work on Document1 docx typing saving They switch to Chrome to handle an email regarding an Annual Meeting They switch to Acrobat to review Report Draft pdf They switch to Excel to update Budget 2024 xlsx They return to Document1 docx to add budget references save and close it They return to Quarterly Report docx type save and close it

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Case Identification Strategy Option A Session based One case for the whole morning Option B Application based One case for Word one for Chrome etc Option C Document Task based Group events by the specific document or task being worked on Alternative Strategy Multi Case Case 1 Document1 docx Case 2 Quarterly Report docx Case 3 Budget 2024 xlsx Case 4 Report Draft pdf Case 5 Email Inbox

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Critique This fragments the timeline too much A user session is often one Case ID in digital footprint analysis Best Approach for Standard Tools Create one Case ID representing the Morning Work Session Session 2024 12 11 01 Revised Approach Group by Document Artifact Lifecycle Case 1 Drafting Document1 docx Case 2 Working on Quarterly Report docx

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: Wait standard Process Mining In BPM a Case is a specific instance of a process Here the Order is the Task Document Final Decision I will treat Document1 docx as the primary Case because it spans the most time and integrates other inputs I will treat Quarterly Report docx as a separate Case Correction Actually the most useful view for a process analyst looking at system logs is to see the flow of work

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refined Logic Case 1 TASK DOC1 Drafting Document 1 Includes the initial work the email the pdf review the budget update and the finalization of Document 1 Case 2 TASK QTR Drafting Quarterly Report Includes the initial focus abandoned and the final work completed The Email PDF Excel happen while Document1 is open but not focused

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Best Practice Case ID Work Session ID Activity Name Standardized Action Context This allows analysis of How long does it take to draft a document including research In Digital Process Mining DPM the session is often the case when analyzing user behavior

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Activity Standardization FOCUS OPEN Switch To TYPING Edit Content SAVE Save Version CLICK Email Open Read Email CLICK Reply Send Send Email SCROLL HIGHLIGHT Review Content CLOSE Close Document

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Self Correction during drafting If I assign Email events to Document1 the process flow for Document1 looks like Open Edit Read Email Review PDF Update Budget Edit Close For Quarterly Report the flow is Open Gap Open Edit Close Decision Case ID SESSION 2024 12 11 AM

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check on Case Logic Assigning support tasks to the main document case is more analyst friendly than having 5 tiny cases The prompt asks for a coherent analyst friendly event log
